settings.title: Settings Center
settings.appearanceHeaderDesc: Customize the look and feel of your GETSSH workspace.
settings.terminalHeaderDesc: Configure your terminal experience and shell behaviors.
settings.sshHeaderDesc: Configure default connection parameters and proxy settings.
settings.systemHeaderDesc: Manage global app behaviors and critical system security modes.
settings.auditHeaderDesc: Review and export secure terminal session records.
settings.devModeDesc: Enable unpacked plugin loading in sandbox
settings.pluginSecurityDesc: Control the isolation level for third-party plugins. Higher security reduces compatibility. Developer mode removes all isolation.
settings.pluginSecurityRestartReq: Please restart GETSSH to clear malicious code from memory and apply Safe Mode.
settings.antiGlareDesc: Enable dark high contrast for terminal text
settings.author: Author
settings.restartDesc: Changes to rendering parameters may require an application restart to take effect.
settings.auditDesc: Read-only history of connection metadata. No keypresses or console output are stored.
settings.exportProfilesDesc: Export all credentials, private key paths, and host configurations into a JSON file. If a master password is set, credentials will be heavily encrypted.
settings.exportHint: Exporting requires a Master Password to protect passwords and private key paths with AES-256 encryption. Hostnames and usernames will be exported in plain text for readability.
settings.exportNeedPwd: Export requires a Master Password. Please set one above in 'SafeStorage Configuration'.
settings.exportTooltipDisabled: Please set a Master Password before exporting
settings.importHint: If the export file is encrypted, enter the master password to decrypt credentials; otherwise, leave blank.
settings.importInvalidFormat: Invalid file format. Please select a GETSSH exported JSON file.
settings.importPwdRequired: This file is encrypted. Please enter the master password.
settings.importSuccessEmpty: ✅ File read successfully, but no new profiles to import.
settings.importStrategyDesc: The database backup you selected contains a Default/Main workspace. You can either completely OVERWRITE your existing data, or MERGE only the external workspaces into your current setup.

security.watchdogStatus: Rust Watchdog Daemon Real-time Status (IPC Ping: {{time}})
security.raspDesc: View the system's underlying security probe and initialization scripts to prevent zero-day injections.
security.privacyDesc: Configure anti-peeping mode, idle auto-lock, and other environmental privacy policies.
security.safeStorageDesc: Manage the current workspace password and enable the highest level AES-256-GCM credential vault.
security.exportDesc: Safely import and export all your connection profiles and application settings.
security.knownHostsDesc: Audit and manage trusted server fingerprints to prevent Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks.
security.encryptionDisabledDesc: The system no longer protects your private keys and passwords. All your credentials will be stored in plain text on the local disk.
security.encryptionEnabledDesc: Your credentials are protected by highly secure AES-256-GCM encryption. If you lose your master password, your keys cannot be recovered.
security.warningPlaintext: DANGER: Once disabled, all your SSH passwords and configs will be stored in plain text on local disk!
security.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke trust for this host? Next connection will require manual verification.
security.errIncorrectPwd: Security Check Failed: Incorrect current password!
security.privacyModeDesc: When GETSSH loses focus, its window will be heavily blurred.
security.autoLockTimeoutDesc: Automatically lock GETSSH after a period of inactivity to prevent unauthorized access.
security.globalInitScriptDesc: Commands to automatically execute sequentially when connecting to any session.
security.pwdUpdated: ✅ Master password securely updated and profiles re-encrypted!
security.pwdDisabled: ⚠️ Encryption disabled. Profiles are now stored in plain text.
security.shieldClickToDetails: Click to view security protection details
security.shieldDetailsTitle: Six Pillars Protecting Your Data Security
security.shieldDetailsIntro: A bottom-up defense-in-depth architecture. Six independent security barriers ensure your connections, keys, and data remain absolutely secure.
security.shieldDetailsWatchdogDesc: An independent daemon. If the main process is unexpectedly suspended, it bypasses V8 to forcefully terminate the polluted process via OS APIs.
security.shieldDetailsZeroizeDesc: Credentials are wiped with 0x00 at the physical memory level via Rust's Zeroize immediately after use, preventing Memory Dump attacks.
security.shieldDetailsCryptoDesc: AES-256-GCM encryption powered by N-API. Combined with robust PBKDF2 key derivation, it eliminates plaintext residue risks caused by V8 GC.
security.shieldDetailsNetworkDesc: SFTP I/O is handled directly by Rust N-API. Zero-copy buffering bypasses the V8 heap, eliminating OOM risks and GC stutters for large files.
security.shieldDetailsRaspDesc: Runtime Application Self-Protection blocks and alerts on unauthorized actions or dangerous commands (like rm -rf) triggered by malicious code.
security.shieldDetailsMemoryDesc: Uses kernel-level privileges to actively scan and detect dynamic injections (e.g. Ptrace) from other processes targeting the main process.
security.pollutedDesc: The system has been polluted. Some security measures are disabled. Please check for abnormal processes immediately.

appearance.glassmorphismOnlyDark: Glassmorphism is only available in Dark Mode
appearance.importFail: Failed to parse theme. Please ensure it's a valid JSON format.

terminal.fontFamilyDesc: Font family used in terminal (e.g. Fira Code, monospace)
terminal.scrollbackDesc: Number of history lines kept in memory
terminal.scrollbackNote: Note: Modifying scrollback typically only applies to new tabs.
terminal.copyOnSelectDesc: Automatically copy highlighted text to system clipboard
terminal.terminalPaddingDesc: Inner spacing of the terminal container (0-32px)
terminal.cursorBlinkDesc: Enable or disable cursor blinking animation

ssh.keepAliveDesc: Periodic heartbeats to prevent timeouts (0 to disable).
ssh.copyOnSelectDesc: Automatically copy highlighted text to system clipboard.

system.confirmQuitDesc: Show confirmation dialog before fully closing the application.
system.globalHotkeyDesc: Press this anywhere to show or hide the terminal (Supports: Command, Control, Option, Shift).

plugins.dropzone: Drag and drop plugin .zip files here to install
plugins.installSuccess: ✅ Plugin "{{name}}" installed successfully! Restart to activate.
plugins.lifecycleGateError: Security blocked: This plugin has not declared a lifecycle hook. Installation rejected. Please ask the plugin developer to add "lifecycle" to getssh.capabilities in package.json.
plugins.uninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to uninstall "{{name}}"?
plugins.uninstallSuccess: 🗑️ Plugin uninstalled. Restart GETSSH to clear UI artifacts.
plugins.permissionDesc: You are about to install {{name}}. Please review the requested capabilities before proceeding.

caps.sshWrite: Can inject commands into active SSH sessions (Warning)
caps.sshRead: Can read output from active SSH sessions (Safe)
caps.hostClipboard: Can read and write to your system clipboard (Warning)

common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this session? This cannot be undone.

welcome.subtitle: Access your remote infrastructure, launch tools, or start a new connection instantly.

midnight: It's late. You should get some rest.
The stars are bright, but your screen is brighter. Take care of your eyes.
Debugging in the dark? Remember to take a break.
Silence all around, only the sound of your keyboard typing.
morning: Good morning! A new day begins.
Rise and shine! May your code be bug-free today.
Early bird catches the worm. Time to build something great!
First coffee, then coding. Let's get started.
forenoon: The day is young and full of possibilities. Code on!
Perfect time to tackle those complex algorithms.
Keep the momentum going, you're doing great!
noon: Good noon. Remember to eat lunch.
Time for a break! Even servers need downtime.
Grab a bite and recharge your brain.
Midday already? Time flies when you're writing elegant code.
afternoon: Good afternoon! Keep up the good work.
The afternoon slump? Not for you! Keep pushing.
Great time for code reviews and optimizations.
evening: The sun has set, but the server never sleeps.
Time to wrap up and reflect on today's commits.
A peaceful evening is perfect for designing architectures.
lateNight: It's getting late. Mind your eyes.
Late night coding sessions are where the magic happens.
The world sleeps, but the hacker remains awake.
Don't let the bugs bite. Try again tomorrow.

about.title: About GETSSH
about.author: Author: RealmCloud Open Source Lab. BY Undertow

aiSettings.overviewDesc: Configure your intelligent co-pilot and AI-driven workspace assistants.

aiSettings.apiKeyDesc: Keys are encrypted locally and never transmitted to GETSSH telemetry servers.

aiSettings.fetchError: Failed to fetch models from the provider endpoint.
aiSettings.manualOverrideDesc: If auto-fetching fails, manually specify the model string here.
aiSettings.subtitle: Intelligence engine

aiSettings.personaSystemPromptPlaceholder: You are a senior database architect...

workspaceCenter.setMainConfirm: Set [{{name}}] as the Main Workspace? The application will automatically boot into this workspace.
workspaceCenter.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete workspace [{{name}}] and ALL its data? This action is irreversible!
workspaceCenter.vaultEncryptedDesc: Your assets will be physically locked using AES-256-GCM. If you lose the master key, the workspace is unrecoverable.
workspaceCenter.vaultPlaintextDesc: Assets will be stored in plaintext or default keychain. Fast access but extreme low security.
workspaceCenter.title: Workspace Center

connection.strictHostKeyDesc: Reject connection if host key changes (prevents MITM)
connection.themeOverrideDesc: Assign a unique color theme to this specific connection to easily distinguish it.
connection.moreAppearanceDesc: Font ligatures, custom backgrounds, and opacity per-host are under development.

protocol.label: Protocol
protocol.autoDesc: Auto-detects protocol from input. Supports ssh:// telnet:// user@host formats.
protocol.localHint: Launches the host system's default shell. No network configuration required.
protocol.telnetHint: Plaintext Telnet, suitable for managing Cisco/Huawei network devices. Terminal type is auto-locked to vt100.
protocol.tooltipAuto: Recommended. Automatically detects and matches the correct protocol based on your input or URL prefix (ssh://, telnet://).
protocol.tooltipSsh: Used for remote Linux/Unix servers and cloud instances (AWS, Alibaba Cloud, Tencent Cloud). Encrypted and highly secure. Most common.
protocol.tooltipLocal: Launches your machine's native shell within a tab. macOS opens Zsh/Bash; Windows opens PowerShell/CMD. No network required.
protocol.tooltipTelnet: Used for legacy network hardware (Cisco, Huawei switches/routers/firewalls). Transmits in cleartext — recommended for trusted intranets or hardware debugging only.

placeholder.host: IP address or domain name, e.g. 192.168.1.100
